The men of Judah did not object to giving up cities within their territory when they were convinced that they had more than was fair. If a true believer has gained an unintended and improper advantage in something, he will give it up without complaint. Love does not seek its own, and does not behave inappropriately; it will lead those in whom it richly dwells to share what they have to supply what is lacking for their brothers and sisters.

In the division to each tribe of Israel, Jacob’s prophetic blessings were fulfilled. They chose for themselves, or it was divided to them by lot, in the manner and places that he foresaw. The word of prophecy is a sure guide: we see by it what to believe, and it proves beyond all doubt the things that are of God.

Joshua waited until all the tribes were settled before he asked for any provision for himself. He was content to be unsettled until he saw them all placed, and in this is an example to all in public positions, to prefer the common good before private advantage. Those who labor most to do good to others seek an inheritance in the heaven above; but it will be soon enough to enter there when they have done all the service to their brothers and sisters of which they are capable. Nor can anything more effectively assure them of their title to it than by trying to bring others to desire, to seek, and to obtain it. Our Lord Jesus came and lived on earth, not in luxury but poverty, providing rest for people, yet himself not having a place to stay; for Christ did not please himself. Nor would he enter upon his inheritance until by his obedience to death he secured the eternal inheritance for all his people; nor will he consider his own glory complete until every redeemed sinner is given possession of their heavenly rest.
